claims.summary: Artifact cleanup uses brittle repeated string replacement that can erase or create marker patterns.
claims.disposition: Current source supersedes that mechanism with a single-pass longest-raw-marker scanner and explicit no-second-order accounting.

claims.summary: Ordinary linguistic substrings and semantic remainder must survive artifact cleanup.
claims.disposition: Verified by common-language, delicate-context, dense-structure, and erased-output regressions.

claims.summary: Nested quoted artifact markers need exact contextual and quoted accounting.
claims.disposition: Implemented and verified with a nested straight-and-smart-quote regression in run 13.

claims.summary: A token-aware parser is needed instead of blind replacement.
claims.disposition: Verified as already implemented by the current scanner; no live parser, prompt, or marker-contract retune was necessary.
